frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5
frameworks.description: Federal control catalog; OverWatch's crosswalk spine — every check carries a NIST 800-53 control.

frameworks.name: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark
frameworks.description: AWS-specific hardening benchmark; the native CIS tags on OverWatch checks.

frameworks.name: PCI DSS
frameworks.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (native per-check tags).

frameworks.name: HIPAA Security Rule
frameworks.description: US ePHI safeguards (native per-check tags).

frameworks.name: SOC 2 (TSC 2017)
frameworks.description: AICPA Trust Services Criteria (native per-check tags).

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (Annex A)
frameworks.description: International ISMS certification standard; Annex A lists 93 security controls across 4 themes (5 organizational, 6 people, 7 physical, 8 technological). Needed by any org seeking ISO 27001 certification or asserting an internationally recognized ISMS.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27002:2022
frameworks.description: Companion code of practice giving implementation guidance for the same 93 controls as ISO 27001:2022 Annex A, numbered as clauses 5.x-8.x (no 'A.' prefix). Used by any org implementing the ISO 27001 control set.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 (Cloud security)
frameworks.description: Code of practice for information security controls for cloud services; adds cloud-specific implementation guidance to ISO/IEC 27002:2013 controls plus 7 new cloud (CLD.x) controls. Used by cloud service providers/customers. NOTE: 27017 uses ISO/IEC 27002:2013 clause numbering (9.x/12.x/13.x) plus CLD.x extensions.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27018:2019 (PII in public cloud)
frameworks.description: Code of practice for protection of personally identifiable information (PII) in public clouds acting as PII processors; extends ISO/IEC 27002:2013 with PII-specific guidance and additional Annex A controls. Only NIST controls with a clear PII-protection nexus are mapped. Uses ISO/IEC 27002:2013 clause numbering.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27701:2019 (PIMS)
frameworks.description: Privacy Information Management System (PIMS) extension to ISO/IEC 27001/27002. Its Clause 6 requires the full ISO 27002 security-control set (numbered 6.x, mirroring ISO/IEC 27002:2013 structure) with privacy interpretations; privacy-specific controls live in Clause 7 (controllers) and Clause 8 (processors). NIST controls map to the Clause-6 ISMS references; the PIMS value-add is the privacy overlay.

frameworks.name: FedRAMP Moderate Baseline
frameworks.description: US federal cloud authorization baseline; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 tailored to the Moderate impact level, required for CSPs handling federal data.

frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-171 Revision 3
frameworks.description: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) in nonfederal systems; requirements are a tailored subset of NIST 800-53 Moderate, so mappings are authoritative and mostly high-confidence.

frameworks.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2
frameworks.description: DoD Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification for defense contractors handling CUI; Level 2 equals the 110 practices of NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2, so practice IDs use the DOMAIN.L2-3.x.x form.

frameworks.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
frameworks.description: Voluntary cross-sector outcome-based framework (Functions/Categories/Subcategories); 800-53 controls are official Informative References so mappings resolve to CSF 2.0 subcategories.

frameworks.name: PCI DSS
frameworks.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard — mandatory for any entity that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data (merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, service providers).

frameworks.name: SWIFT Customer Security Controls Framework (CSCF)
frameworks.description: Mandatory security baseline for institutions connected to the SWIFT financial-messaging network (Customer Security Programme); 32 controls (26 mandatory, 6 advisory).

frameworks.name: NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 500
frameworks.description: New York State Department of Financial Services cybersecurity regulation binding on banks, insurers, and other DFS-licensed financial institutions ('Covered Entities').

frameworks.name: EU Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA)
frameworks.description: EU regulation on ICT risk management and operational resilience, binding on EU financial entities (banks, insurers, investment firms, crypto/payment providers) and their critical ICT third parties; detailed technical controls sit in RTS (EU) 2024/1774.

frameworks.name: MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines
frameworks.description: Monetary Authority of Singapore guidelines setting technology and cyber risk-management expectations for Singapore-regulated financial institutions; 13 sections / 244 guideline statements.

frameworks.name: HIPAA Security Rule
frameworks.version: 45 CFR Part 164 Subpart C (2013 Omnibus); crosswalk via NIST SP 800-66 Rev. 2 (2024)
frameworks.description: US federal safeguards standard for electronic protected health information (ePHI); required of HIPAA covered entities and business associates. Crosswalk anchored to NIST SP 800-66 Rev. 2 Appendix D (HIPAA standard -> 800-53r5).

frameworks.name: HITRUST CSF
frameworks.description: Certifiable control framework widely used by healthcare and health-tech; harmonizes ISO 27001/27002, NIST 800-53, HIPAA and others. HITRUST publishes authoritative NIST 800-53 -> CSF mappings in MyCSF; control references follow the domain.control (e.g., 01.c, 09.aa) convention.

frameworks.name: General Data Protection Regulation
frameworks.description: EU regulation on processing of personal data; applies to any organization processing EU residents' data. Principles-based, so technical controls map chiefly to Article 32 (security of processing), with supporting Articles 5, 25, 30, 33; confidence is bounded by GDPR not being a technical control catalog.

frameworks.name: CCPA / CPRA
frameworks.version: CCPA (2018) as amended by CPRA (2020), operative 2023
frameworks.description: California consumer-privacy statute (Civil Code 1798.100 et seq.). Primarily a rights/obligations law; the security hook is the duty to maintain 'reasonable security procedures and practices' (1798.100(e)), the breach private right of action (1798.150(a)), and CPRA cybersecurity-audit/risk-assessment rulemaking (1798.185(a)(15)). Most technical controls map to reasonable security at low confidence; the CA AG has treated the CIS Controls as defining 'reasonable security.'

frameworks.name: PIPEDA (Canada)
frameworks.version: S.C. 2000, c. 5 — Schedule 1 (CSA Model Code, 10 fair-information principles)
frameworks.description: Canadian federal private-sector privacy law. Security maps almost entirely to Principle 4.7 (Safeguards) and its sub-clauses (4.7.3 names technological measures such as passwords and encryption); accountability/procedures under Principle 4.1. Principles-based, so most mappings are low confidence.

frameworks.name: CSA Cloud Controls Matrix
frameworks.description: Cloud-specific control framework (17 domains, 197 control specifications) for cloud providers and customers; foundation for CSA STAR certification.

frameworks.name: AWS Well-Architected Framework - Security Pillar
frameworks.description: AWS best-practice guidance for securing cloud workloads, organized as questions SEC 1-11 with best practices; applied in Well-Architected Reviews.

frameworks.name: AWS Foundational Technical Review
frameworks.description: AWS Partner Network checklist for ISV/SaaS solutions covering security, reliability, and operational best practices required before co-sell/listing. (Mappings are domain-level, not per-control — treated as low-confidence.)

frameworks.name: BSI Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue (C5)
frameworks.description: German Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) audited attestation criteria for cloud providers; 17 domains, 121 basic criteria. (Mappings are domain-level, not per-control — treated as low-confidence.)

frameworks.name: MITRE ATT&CK for Cloud/IaaS
frameworks.description: Adversary technique knowledge base; each control mapped to the Cloud/IaaS technique(s) it mitigates, per CTID NIST 800-53 Rev5 -> ATT&CK mappings.

frameworks.name: CIS Critical Security Controls
frameworks.description: Prioritized set of 18 controls / 153 safeguards for general cyber defense; a widely adopted baseline for organizations of all sizes.

frameworks.name: CIS Amazon Web Services Foundations Benchmark
frameworks.description: AWS account-hardening benchmark (~60 recommendations across IAM, Storage, Logging, Monitoring, Networking); the de-facto baseline for securing AWS environments.

frameworks.name: COBIT 2019
frameworks.description: ISACA IT governance and management framework; 40 governance & management objectives across 5 domains (EDM, APO, BAI, DSS, MEA). Maps at a process/governance altitude, so most crosswalks are analogous rather than control-for-control.

frameworks.name: UK Cyber Essentials
frameworks.description: UK NCSC/IASME baseline of 5 technical control themes (Firewalls, Secure Configuration, User Access Control, Malware Protection, Security Update Management) for basic cyber hygiene; required for many UK government contracts.

frameworks.name: ACSC Essential Eight
frameworks.description: Australian Signals Directorate baseline of 8 prioritized mitigation strategies (application control, patch applications, Office macro settings, user application hardening, restrict admin privileges, patch OS, MFA, regular backups) with maturity levels 0-3. Deliberately narrow, so many NIST controls have no equivalent.

frameworks.name: EU NIS2 Directive (Directive (EU) 2022/2555)
frameworks.description: EU-wide cybersecurity risk-management law for essential/important entities; Article 21(2) enumerates 10 minimum measures (a-j). Needed by orgs operating in critical/important sectors across the EU.

frameworks.name: ISO 22301:2019 Business Continuity Management Systems
frameworks.description: ISO management-system standard for business continuity (BCMS). Overlaps our technical control set only on backup/recovery; needed by orgs certifying BC resilience.

frameworks.name: UK NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework (CAF)
frameworks.description: Outcome-based UK assurance framework (4 objectives, 14 principles, 39 contributing outcomes) for operators of essential services / critical national infrastructure and UK government (GovAssure).

frameworks.name: TISAX (VDA ISA 6.0)
frameworks.description: ENX/VDA automotive-industry information-security assessment; Information Security module derived from ISO/IEC 27001/27002:2022. Required of OEM suppliers/partners handling automotive data.

frameworks.name: ISA/IEC 62443-3-3 System Security Requirements and Security Levels
frameworks.description: System-level security requirements for industrial automation & control systems (IACS), organized by 7 Foundational Requirements. Applies to OT/ICS environments; NIST SP 800-82r3 provides the authoritative 800-53 <-> 62443 overlay.
